problem.title: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
problem.description: A trie (pronounced as 'try') or prefix tree is a tree data structure used to efficiently store and retrieve keys in a dataset of strings. There are various applications of this data structure, such as autocomplete and spellchecker.

example.input: insert("apple"), search("apple") → true, search("app") → false, startsWith("app") → true, insert("app"), search("app") → true
example.explanation: Trie stores strings by prefix. search checks complete word; startsWith checks any prefix.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Trie Implementation [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Each TrieNode stores a map from characters to child nodes and a boolean marking whether it ends a word. Insert walks the path letter by letter, creating nodes as needed and setting the end-of-word flag at the last node. Search and startsWith both walk the same path but differ only in whether they check the end-of-word flag, giving O(m) time per operation where m is the word length.

complexity.time: O(m) for insert/search/startsWith where m is word length
complexity.space: O(ALPHABET_SIZE * m * n) where n is number of words
